summary.intent: Address a Greptile code review comment requiring tests for retry logic in the Matrix client crate.
summary.outcome: Extracted retry loop into a testable generic helper, added 5 unit tests covering backoff/cancellation behavior, fixed a tokio test-util feature gap, and all 100 matrix crate tests pass.

repo: CLAUDE.md mandates high test coverage — Greptile enforces this as a P1 issue.
The Matrix client's retry logic was originally coupled to the real Matrix SDK's sync() call and CancellationToken, requiring extraction before unit testing was feasible.
tokio's 'full' feature set does NOT include 'test-util' — start_paused = true on #[tokio::test] requires an explicit test-util feature in dev-dependencies.

code.finding: retry_loop was extracted into a standalone async fn accepting a generic future-producing closure so it can be tested without a real Matrix client.
tokio dev-dependency needed explicit 'test-util' feature added alongside 'full' to support #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)].

workflow: jq can fail with parse errors on multiline/control-char-containing API responses when polling CI check status — direct API calls are more reliable than piped polling scripts.
Rust select! macro branches must return the same type; discarding return values with _ is the idiomatic fix when branch outputs differ.
Greptile re-review requires an explicit trigger after pushing fixes, not just waiting for CI.

friction: Polling script for Greptile check status failed due to jq parse error on multiline response text, requiring a manual fallback to direct API check.
Multiple compilation iterations needed: generic return type on retry_loop, select! branch type mismatch, missing Future import.
tokio 'full' feature giving a misleading error — the actual missing feature (test-util) was not named in the compiler error, pointing instead at assertion lines.
